texto: Language is accurate: you run for your life. If you are dying, leave. If you are suffering, move. There is no other law, only movement.
explicacao: Citação de Amélie Nothomb sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Language is accurate: you run for your life. If you are dying, leave. If you are suffering, move.…” articula reflexao, move-on, movement em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como movement e suffering.

texto: If a writer manages to be fascinating about his own novels, then there are only two possibilities: either he is merely voicing out loud what he wrote in his book, and he is a parrot; or he is explaining interesting things that he didn't discuss in his book, in which case the book in question is a failure since it does not live up to its claims.
explicacao: Citação de Amélie Nothomb sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “If a writer manages to be fascinating about his own novels, then there are only two possibilities…” articula reflexao, writers-on-writing, writer em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: There is only one way to overcome the difficulty of writing, and that is to write. Thought only becomes effective and productive at the time of writing.

texto: Behind every work of art lies the enormous pretension of exhibiting one's vision of the world. If such obvious arrogance is not counterbalanced by the tribulations of doubt, all that remains is a monster who is to art what a fanatic is to faith.
explicacao: Citação de Amélie Nothomb sobre temas ligados a fe. “Behind every work of art lies the enormous pretension of exhibiting one's vision of the world. If…” articula fe, arrogance, art em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e art.

texto: My mind was not that of a conqueror, but that of a cow that spends its life chewing contentedly in the meadow of invoices, waiting for the train of eternal grace to pass by. How good it felt to exist without pride of ambition. To live in hibernation.

texto: What is a flower? A giant sexual organ in its Sunday best. The truth has been known for a long time, yet, over-aged adolescents that we are, we persist in speaking sentimental drives about the delicacy of flowers. We construct idiotic phrases like "So-and-so is in the flower of his youth", which is as absurd as saying "in the vagina of his youth".
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Amélie Nothomb: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “What is a flower? A giant sexual organ in its Sunday best. The truth has been known for a long ti…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, flowers, flower pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: It is too sad . I must speak to him- The do you really ?- Sure . How can you expect things to get better , if we do not speak?- Earlier , you talked to Mr. Omochi . Do you feel that things have thus been arranged?- What is certain is that if we do not talk , there is no chance to solve the problem.- What seems more certain is that if we talk, there is serious risk of aggravating the situation.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Amélie Nothomb que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “It is too sad . I must speak to him- The do you really ?- Sure . How can you expect things to get…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, comunicacion, encuentros, com foco em encuentros e palabras.
